title: Java Enum Members
description: Practice enum fields, constructors, and methods.
learningGoals: Recall that enum constructors are not public and run once per constant.
Recognize that enum constants can carry fields and behavior.

choices: Compilation fails because enums cannot declare methods
completionChecks.explanation: HIGH is constructed with priority 10, and urgent() returns priority >= 10, so Level.HIGH.urgent() is true; enums may declare fields and methods.

completionChecks.prompt: Why is Level(int priority) not public?
choices: Enum constructors cannot be public; they run once per constant
Enums cannot have constructors at all
Only abstract enums can have fields
Because the priority field is final
completionChecks.explanation: Enum constructors are implicitly private and never public; they run once for each declared constant, and callers use the constants rather than new.
MEDIUM stores priority 5, and urgent only returns true when the stored priority is at least 10.
